name: Ojos de Monreal del Río Jiloca — ida y vuelta desde Villafranca del Campo
region_intro: Mountain biking around Villafranca Del Campo offers diverse terrain within the Jiloca valley, characterized by extensive agricultural fields and remnants of holm oak forests. The region's landscape is shaped by the Jiloca River and its tributaries, with the western part featuring the Rambla del Valle and foothills of Sierra Menera. This area provides a mix of accessible paths through cultivated lands and more varied terrain leading to the broader Teruel province, known for its forests and high-mountain environments. Riders can expect routes that traverse both open plains and areas with natural tree cover. Best mountain bike trails around Villafranca Del Campo The most popular mountain bike trail is Sierra Menera BTT 02 Route, a 31.7 miles (51.1 km) trail that takes 4 hours 18 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the varied landscapes around Sierra Menera. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Sierra Lidón — circular desde Monreal del Campo (BTT03), a moderate 38.4 miles (61.9 km) path. This circular route offers extensive views and diverse terrain around Sierra Lidón. Local mountain bikers also love the Ojos de Monreal del Río Jiloca — ida y vuelta desde Villafranca del Campo, an 18.7 miles (30.0 km) trail leading through the Jiloca River valley, often completed in about 1 hour 57 minutes. Mountain biking around Villafranca Del Campo is defined by the Jiloca valley's agricultural plains, remnants of holm oak forests, and the diverse terrains of the Teruel province. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy valley rides to more challenging routes in the surrounding hills. The routes in Villafranca Del Campo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.0 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 50 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Villafranca Del Campo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when mountain biking around Villafranca del Campo?
A: The terrain around Villafranca del Campo is quite varied. You'll find extensive agricultural fields and remnants of holm oak forests in the Jiloca valley. The western part features the Rambla del Valle and the foothills of Sierra Menera. This mix provides both accessible paths through cultivated lands and more challenging routes leading into the broader Teruel province, which is known for its forests and high-mountain environments.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in the area?
A: Yes, there is at least one easy mountain bike trail available. For a relatively flat and scenic ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004903398/ojos-de-monreal-del-rio-jiloca-ida-y-vuelta-desde-villafranca-del-campo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos de Monreal del Río Jiloca — ida y vuelta desde Villafranca del Campo&lt;/a&gt;. This 30.0 km route follows the Jiloca River valley and has minimal elevation gain, making it a good option for those new to mountain biking.

Q: What are some longer, more challenging mountain bike routes near Villafranca del Campo?
A: For a more extensive ride, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1003685330/sierra-lidon-circular-desde-monreal-del-campo-btt03" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Lidón — circular desde Monreal del Campo (BTT03)&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 61.9 km path offering extensive views and diverse terrain. Another moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1003677756/sierra-menera-btt-02-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Menera BTT 02 Route&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 51.1 km and explores the varied landscapes around Sierra Menera.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, several routes are circular. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1003685330/sierra-lidon-circular-desde-monreal-del-campo-btt03" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Lidón — circular desde Monreal del Campo (BTT03)&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular route. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541524" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monreal del Campo – Caño el Gato loop from Monreal del Campo&lt;/a&gt;, which is also a moderate circular trail.

Q: What historical landmarks or attractions can I see while mountain biking in the area?
A: The region offers several historical points of interest. You could visit the restored 16th-17th century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4852508" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos Negros Mill&lt;/a&gt;, or the impressive 13th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3925682" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peracense Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which provides spectacular views. Villafranca del Campo is also situated on the historical &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1263070" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of El Cid&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any natural features or viewpoints to look out for on the trails?
A: Absolutely. The Jiloca valley itself, with its river and agricultural plains, offers scenic views. You can also find remnants of holm oak groves, such as the 'Carrascal de la Matilla,' particularly towards the foothills of Sierra Menera. The broader Teruel province, accessible from Villafranca del Campo, features diverse landscapes including ochre and reddish rock formations, narrow canyons, and sweeping pine forests, especially in areas like the Sierra de Albarracín.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails around Villafranca del Campo?
A: The mountain biking routes around Villafranca del Campo are generally well-regarded by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.0 stars from over 3 reviews. Riders often appreciate the varied terrain, which includes both open plains and areas with natural tree cover, and the options available for different ability levels.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in the Villafranca del Campo area?
A: There are around 13 mountain bike trails documented in the Villafranca del Campo area, offering a range of experiences from easy valley rides to more challenging routes in the surrounding hills.

Q: Is the area suitable for mountain biking with families?
A: Yes, the presence of easy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004903398/ojos-de-monreal-del-rio-jiloca-ida-y-vuelta-desde-villafranca-del-campo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos de Monreal del Río Jiloca — ida y vuelta desde Villafranca del Campo&lt;/a&gt;, which follows a relatively flat river valley, makes the area suitable for families looking for a less strenuous cycling experience. The agricultural roads and paths through the Jiloca valley are generally accessible.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Villafranca del Campo?
A: Given its location in the Jiloca valley and the broader Teruel province, spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for mountain biking. The weather is milder, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder temperatures of winter, making for more comfortable riding conditions and vibrant landscapes.

Q: Are there any other notable towns or cultural sites near the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, the nearby settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5058102" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monreal del Campo&lt;/a&gt; is historically significant and known for its saffron museum. Exploring these towns can add a cultural dimension to your outdoor adventure.
tips: Next to Cerro de la Cruz and a few kilometers from Villafranca del Campo, we find these beautiful views that will accompany us during a good part of our route if we head towards the Sierra Lidón

Near the town of Monreal del Campo, we will find this beautiful wetland where we will enjoy walking along its paths accompanied by the water.
We can also stop to recover energy at the tables that are at the entrance from the road.
We are in the town of Monreal del Campo, located in the Jiloca region where we can visit the beautiful church, the Saffron Museum or near the town, the Ojos del Jiloca or Ojos de Monreal.
